title: taraxacum parile
intro: Taraxacum parile, also known as the lesser dandelion, is a plant species that belongs to the dandelion family and is characterized by its smaller size and less aggressive growth compared to the common dandelion. The medicinal properties of Taraxacum parile include its diuretic, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, which can help to relieve digestive issues and soothe skin irritations. The plant is also rich in vitamins A, B, and C, as well as minerals like potassium and iron, making it a valuable source of nutrition and health benefits. Taraxacum parile can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in herbal remedies to support liver function, reduce bloating, and promote overall well-being.
